mnemonics: Q.U.A.L.I.T.Y. - Quantifiable, Usable, Accessible, Logical, Integrated, Tested, Yielding value
keyPoints: Quality is conformance to requirements, not just 'goodness'.
Quality must be built into the process from the start; it cannot be tested in at the end.
Quality attributes (e.g., reliability, usability, performance) are non-functional requirements that define system behavior.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on functional requirements while neglecting non-functional quality attributes.
Treating quality assurance as a separate final phase rather than an integrated, ongoing activity.
